question_text: A plastic rod rubbed with a cloth becomes negatively charged. Which of these best describes what happened to the cloth?

options.label: A
options.text: The cloth became positively charged because electrons moved from the rod to the cloth

options.label: B
options.text: The cloth became positively charged because electrons moved from the cloth to the rod

options.label: C
options.text: The cloth remained neutral because charge cannot transfer between insulators

options.label: D
options.text: The cloth became negatively charged to balance the rod

correct_answer: The cloth became positively charged because electrons moved from the cloth to the rod
explanation: When a plastic rod is rubbed with a cloth, electrons transfer from the cloth to the rod. The rod gains electrons and becomes negatively charged, while the cloth loses electrons and becomes positively charged. This demonstrates that charge is conserved - the total charge remains zero, but it is redistributed between the two materials.
